Discovery: Down 42% from 52w-high with RSI 38.7 — a profitable, dividend-paying consumer staples compounder punished by commodity cost spikes tied to Middle East (Iran war) tensions; Q4 FY2026 earnings (fiscal year ends May 31, report expected late June/July 2026) provides near-term reset catalyst, and any geopolitical de-escalation normalizes commodity costs directly. Bear case: food inflation and private-label substitution are structural headwinds; management attributed headwinds to 'heightened volatility in certain commodity markets due to the war in Iran' — no control over that timeline.
growth_picks.analyst_take: At 30-year price lows with 10.4% dividend yield but Wells Fargo Underweight $15 and structural volume decline is secular
